title: Key Characteristics of an Effective Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
summary: Outlines the essential attributes of a good WBS, including its hierarchical structure, deliverable focus, the 100 % rule, and its role as a foundation for project planning and control.
content: A good WBS is hierarchical, representing all deliverables for physical products, services, or processes. It may include project methodology deliverables, often in a separate methodology WBS. Each level adds granularity, with child layers clarifying parent deliverables. The WBS must be complete (100% rule) and agreed upon by all stakeholders. It serves as the foundation for defining activities, schedules, budgets, and scope‑creep control. The WBS is applicable to any project type because every project produces deliverables.
can_answer: What are the key characteristics of a Work Breakdown Structure?
Why is hierarchy important in a WBS?
How does the 100 % rule apply to a WBS?
What role does a WBS play in project planning and control?
